Appeal for witnesses

Witnesses to the recent incident involving the TranzAlpine train service that was disabled in a tunnel are asked to provide a statement. 

 

Read more

Search Results

1-12 of 1171 results
Rail RO-2026-104

Passenger train disabled in tunnel, near Springfield, 30 May 2026

The Commission is investigating an incident involving the disablement of a passenger train in a tunnel.

Incident date: 30 May 2026 Publish date: Not yet published
In progress
Maritime MO-2026-203

Fishing vessel Lucy-C, capsize, Peketa, near Kaikōura, 25 May 2026  

The Commission is investigating a fatal accident involving the fishing vessel Lucy C that occurred off Peketa, Kaikoura at about 0700 on 25 May 2026. The reported circumstances were that the Lucy C was underway fishing craypots near shore with two people onboard, when the vessel capsized. One crew member swam to the nearby beach, with minor injuries. The skipper was later located at a nearby beach, deceased.

Incident date: 25 May 2026 Publish date: Not yet published
In progress
Aviation AO-2026-002

Airbus AS350B3 helicopter, collision with terrain, west of Makarora, 17 May 2026

The Commission is inquiring into an accident on 17 May 2026 involving a commercially operated Airbus AS350B3 helicopter, registration ZK-IJW. The reported circumstances were that the helicopter crashed into a mountainside in Mount Aspiring National Park approximately 24 kilometres west of Makarora, north of Wanaka. Reportedly, there were two fatalities and three passengers with minor injuries.

Incident date: 17 May 2026 Publish date: Not yet published
In progress
Rail RO-2026-103

Freight train, derailment, Dunedin, 16 April 2026

The Commission is investigating a reported pair of derailments that involved the same freight train on the Main South Line the night of 16 April 2026. Reportedly, the first occurred near Allanton and the second in the Dunedin rail yard. Both derailments reportedly involved the train’s rolling stock, which remained upright but sustained serious damage. There were no injuries to people.

Incident date: 16 Apr 2026 Publish date: Not yet published
In progress
Maritime MO-2026-202

Person overboard, FV Amaltal Mariner, South Pacific, 5 April 2026

The Commission is investigating a person overboard from the commercial New Zealand-flagged fishing vessel, Amaltal Mariner in the South Pacific Ocean. The reported circumstances were that at approximately 2240 on 5 April 2026, a crew member was knocked overboard by a float while deploying fishing gear. Reportedly the vessel was approximately 215 nautical miles east of the Auckland Islands. Immediate efforts did not locate the missing crew member and at time of opening this inquiry they remained unaccounted for.

Incident date: 05 Apr 2026 Publish date: Not yet published
In progress
Rail RO-2026-102

Safe working irregularity, track workers and freight train, Dunedin, 24 March 2026

The Commission is investigating a safe working irregularity just North of Dunedin Railway Station. The reported circumstances included that a freight train entered a worksite after incorrect removal of blocking on the Main South Line between Dunedin and Sawyers Bay - resulting in a near miss between the freight train and track workers operating a Hi Rail Vehicle.

Incident date: 24 Mar 2026 Publish date: Not yet published
In progress
Rail RO-2026-101

Freight train collided with private motor vehicle at level crossing, near New Plymouth, 25 February 2026

The Commission is investigating a collision between a freight train and a private motor vehicle on the Marton to New Plymouth Line on 25 February 2026. The reported circumstances were that a loaded northbound freight train and a car collided at a railway level crossing near Lepperton. The car’s two occupants were seriously injured and there was extensive damage to the car.

Incident date: 25 Feb 2026 Publish date: Not yet published
In progress
Aviation AO-2026-001

Boeing 737-8BK, high-speed rejected take off, Melbourne Aerodrome, Australia, 16 February 2026

The Commission is assisting the Australian Transport Safety Bureau’s investigation of an incident involving a Boeing 737-8BK freight aircraft registered ZK-TXB on 16 February 2026 at Melbourne Aerodrome (Tullamarine Airport).

Incident date: 16 Feb 2026 Publish date: Not yet published
In progress
Maritime MO-2026-201

Black Cat grounding, Akaroa Harbour, 31 January 2026

The Commission is investigating an accident involving a commercial passenger vessel grounding in Akaroa Harbour on 31 January 2026.

Incident date: 31 Jan 2026 Publish date: Not yet published
In progress
Aviation AO-2025-016

Bell 206L-3, collision with terrain, near Raetihi, 13 December 2025

The Commission is investigating an accident involving a Bell 206L-3 helicopter approximately 15km southwest of Raetihi at about 7am on 13 December 2025. The reported circumstances were that the helicopter impacted terrain with one fatality, the pilot, who was the sole occupant on board.

Incident date: 13 Dec 2025 Publish date: Not yet published
In progress
Maritime MO-2025-206

FV Taudre, grounding, near French Pass, Marlborough, on 2 December 2025.

TAIC is investigating the grounding of the fishing vessel Taudre, after which the crew abandoned ship, the vessel later refloated and drifted, and additional crew re-boarded with Coastguard assistance to manage flooding from a hull breach. The Taudre was towed to anchorage for assessment.

Incident date: 02 Dec 2025 Publish date: Not yet published
In progress
Aviation AO-2025-015

Airbus A380-861, A6-EUE, damaged tyres and wheels, Auckland, 30 November 2025

At Auckland Aerodrome an Airbus A380-861 damage to two of the aeroplane’s tyres and wheels. No injuries have been reported.

Incident date: 30 Nov 2025 Publish date: Not yet published
In progress
